title: oxalis enneaphylla var. pumila
intro: Oxalis enneaphylla var. pumila, also known as the 'Dwarf Oxalis', is a small perennial plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its delicate white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of oxalic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Oxalis enneaphylla var. pumila include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots and leaves in a decoction, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
